stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Rebecca Belmore is an Anishinaabe multidisciplinary artist.
  • The Anishinaabe are an Indigenous people of North America.
  • Belmore’s installations address themes of identity and place and have appeared in more than 60 exhibitions.
  • Her 2014 installation Trace was displayed at the Canadian Museum for Human Rights.
  • Her 2019 installation Turbulent Water was displayed at the Remai Modern.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the order in which two of Rebecca Belmore’s installations were displayed. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Anishinaabe multidisciplinary artist Rebecca Belmore has presented her work in more than 60 exhibitions, including the installations Trace and Turbulent Water.
Rebecca Belmore’s 2019 installation Turbulent Water was displayed at the Remai Modern; another of her installations appeared at the Canadian Museum for Human Rights.
Rebecca Belmore’s installations, several of which appeared in 2014 and 2019 exhibitions, address themes of identity and place.
Rebecca Belmore’s 2014 installation Trace was displayed first, and five years later her installation Turbulent Water followed.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the order in which two of Belmore’s installations were displayed, indicating that Trace appeared in 2014 and Turbulent Water appeared five years later (2019).

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence mentions two of Belmore’s installations, it doesn’t indicate the order in which they were displayed. Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence mentions two of Belmore’s installations and specifies when one of them was displayed, it doesn’t state when the installation at the Canadian Museum for Human Rights occurred, so the order isn’t established. Choice C is incorrect. While the sentence mentions that exhibitions of Belmore’s installations took place in 2014 and 2019, it doesn’t identify the installations or emphasize the order in which they were displayed.
